resolves_blocking_finding: engine/btree/latch.go:212-234 (Unlock ordering doc-comment claim was untested; TestNodeLatchNoDoubleLockAcrossEviction's stress-based coverage did not catch the reversed ordering when the verifier manually swapped it, 3x, under -race, count=5)
fix_option_chosen: a (deterministic hook-based test), not b (soften comment) -- architecture-discovery.md documents why the ordering claim was found to be real/reachable, not merely theoretical, so softening the comment would have been factually wrong

pointers.new_hook: engine/btree/latch.go: var unlockOrderHook func(nodeID uint64), invoked in Unlock between l.mu.Unlock() and s.releaseLatch(nodeID, l); nil in production, mirrors optimisticReadHook (lookup.go) / crabRetryHook (insert.go) pattern

note_run_dir_collision: 052-implementation was found already claimed by a concurrent agent for issue #39 (mvcc) when this run started; this run used 053-implementation instead and did not touch 052-implementation's contents (its metadata.json shows as modified in the working tree only because of that other agent's own concurrent activity, not this run)
verification_should_check: TestNodeLatchUnlockOrderingPreventsDoubleLock passes deterministically (not just probabilistically) and its doc comment's mutation-test claim is credible from reading the code (it is white-box, uses peekLatch/l.mu.TryLock() directly, which is legitimate within package btree's own test file)
Unlock's doc comment now points at this test as the concrete proof of the ordering claim
no changes outside engine/btree/ in this commit
full engine/btree test suite still green under -race -count=5 (see self-consistency.json)
not_self_verified: per invariant I4, this run does not verify its own work; /cdr:verify should independently confirm the mutation-test claim (e.g. by re-running the reversal itself) before approving
